bead.title: Align HELIX execution contract to shipped DDx execute-loop / execute-bead capabilities
bead.description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>HELIX should consume DDx substrate capabilities rather than shadow-implement them · Treat execute-bead as the canonical bounded execution primitive when DDx ships it · Prefer queue cleanup that closes already-satisfied findings instead of carrying stale review debt</practices>
<governing>CONTRACT-001 · FEAT-011 · TD-011 — HELIX already specifies ddx agent execute-bead as the bounded DDx-managed execution primitive, but live workflow docs and implementation surfaces still mix that contract with direct ddx agent run orchestration.</governing>
</context-digest>

DDX now ships `ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` locally. Align HELIX governing docs and queue policy to that shipped capability, identify the remaining gap between current HELIX implementation and the contract already written in FEAT-011 / TD-011 / CONTRACT-001, and queue any concrete follow-on implementation work needed to start using execute-bead in the HELIX execution path.

Scope:
- Reconcile HELIX governing docs that still describe direct `ddx agent run` orchestration for bounded bead execution.
- Distinguish DDx-managed execute-bead flows from plain `ddx agent run` usage for planning, review, and non-managed prompts.
- Review the outstanding HELIX queue for stale findings already satisfied, findings superseded by execute-bead adoption, and follow-on work that becomes newly relevant once execute-bead is available.
- Create any follow-on build/test beads needed for actual helix-run / helix-build adoption after the contract is aligned.
bead.acceptance: Governing HELIX docs identify `ddx agent execute-loop` as the primary queue-drain substrate and `ddx agent execute-bead` as the bounded execution primitive; HELIX bead guidance requires deterministic success-measurement criteria suitable for DDx-managed close-with-evidence; any remaining implementation gap between current HELIX behavior and the shipped DDx execution surfaces is captured as concrete follow-on beads with deterministic acceptance.
